task_completion.description: Open the rabies virus genome file in UGENE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE application window is open and displays the loaded 'rabies_virus_genome.gb' file, showing the sequence or annotations.

task_completion.description: Translate the CDS annotations into amino acid sequences.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE interface showing the translation of CDS regions, such as an 'Export/Import' dialog configured to translate to amino acids, or a sequence view displaying the amino acid sequences.

task_completion.description: Export the translated amino acid sequences to a FASTA file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: UGENE export dialog showing the destination path '~/UGENE_Data/results/rabies_proteome.fasta' and format set to FASTA, or a terminal/file manager showing the created file.

task_completion.description: Record the total number of exported proteins in the summary text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing 'proteome_summary.txt' containing the number 5 (representing the total proteins).

task_completion.description: Record the name and length of the largest protein in the summary text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing 'proteome_summary.txt' mentioning the 'L protein' (or simply 'L') and its length of '2142'.

task_completion.description: Record the exact length of the Glycoprotein (G) in the summary text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing 'proteome_summary.txt' mentioning the 'Glycoprotein' (or 'G') and its length of '524'.

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE GUI to process the genome rather than bypassing it with terminal commands or Python scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the UGENE application menus, sequence viewer, or export dialogs.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the information from the software rather than blindly copying expected answers from external knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent viewing the protein lengths or annotations within UGENE before writing the summary file.

integrity.description: The agent created the output files in the exact specified directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal, file manager, or text editor paths confirming the files are located in '~/UGENE_Data/results/'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Rabies virus reference genome (NC_001542.1) contains exactly 5 proteins. The largest protein is the L protein, which is 2142 amino acids long. The Glycoprotein (G) is 524 amino acids long. The expected output files are 'rabies_proteome.fasta' and 'proteome_summary.txt' located in the '~/UGENE_Data/results/' directory.
